page: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing AMD EPYC Milan processors running at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a local 200 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity is established with a 1 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities. The stress-ng single-core CPU test places this server in the top 10% tier, while the Geekbench single-core score and bzip3 single-threaded decompression benchmarks rank in the top 25%. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput also align with average tier performance.

The server offers dedicated CPU allocation without hyper-threading, ensuring consistent compute performance for workloads that do not benefit from shared-thread architectures. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a potential constraint for memory-heavy applications. This resource density makes the instance a cost-effective option for compute-bound tasks that require predictable CPU performance without massive memory footprints. Suitable workloads include single-threaded processing, web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.
description: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and a 200 GB local NVMe SSD. The server delivers top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng single-core benchmarks, and strong single-threaded decompression and text processing capabilities. With a 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and dedicated CPU allocation, it offers predictable performance for compute-bound workloads. The 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications, making this server best suited for web serving, software compilation, and single-threaded processing tasks where CPU dedication is prioritized over large memory capacity.
og_description: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c3-16 is a compute-optimized server with 8 AMD EPYC Milan cores, 16 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe storage, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and local NVMe storage for predictable single-threaded performance.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores with no hyper-threading
16.0 GB of system memory and 200 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and single-threaded tasks
